skill.name: react-best-practices
skill.description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. Provides 45 rules across 8 categories for eliminating waterfalls, bundle optimization, server-side performance, client-side data fetching, re-render optimization, rendering performance, JavaScript performance, and advanced patterns.
skill.summary: Comprehensive React and Next.js performance guide with prioritized rules for code generation and refactoring.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 912 patterns but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains markdown documentation with code examples for educational purposes, not executable code. Patterns like 'backtick execution' are shell commands in README examples (pnpm build), 'dynamic imports' are React next/dynamic usage examples, 'storage access' shows localStorage caching patterns, and 'network' patterns are fetch API documentation examples. No actual security vulnerabilities exist.

content.user_title: Optimize React and Next.js Performance with Vercel Best Practices
content.value_statement: Poor React performance leads to slow load times and frustrated users. This skill provides 45 prioritized rules from Vercel Engineering to eliminate waterfalls, reduce bundle sizes, and optimize rendering.
actual_capabilities: Eliminate async waterfalls using Promise.all() and proper await placement
Reduce bundle size with dynamic imports and code splitting strategies
Optimize server-side rendering with React.cache() and LRU caching
Prevent unnecessary re-renders using proper dependency arrays and memoization
Improve client-side data fetching with SWR deduplication patterns
Guide automated refactoring of React/Next.js code for performance
limitations: Focuses on performance optimization, not general React patterns or accessibility
Examples use TypeScript syntax; JavaScript-only projects need adaptation
Some patterns require React 18+ features like Suspense and startTransition
Does not cover testing strategies or debugging performance issues

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Building React Applications
use_cases.description: Use this skill when writing new components or pages to ensure optimal performance from the start. The skill guides bundle optimization, proper data fetching patterns, and re-render prevention techniques.

use_cases.title: Tech Lead Reviewing Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Reference these rules during code review to catch performance anti-patterns like sequential async calls, unnecessary barrel imports, or missing Suspense boundaries before merging.

use_cases.title: AI Assistant Refactoring Legacy Code
use_cases.description: Apply these prioritized rules to systematically improve existing React codebases, starting with critical waterfall elimination before addressing medium-impact rendering optimizations.

prompt_templates.title: Review Component for Performance Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component using Vercel best practices. Identify waterfalls, unnecessary re-renders, and bundle optimization opportunities. Suggest specific fixes with code examples.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Sequential Fetching to Parallel
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this code to eliminate async waterfalls. Use Promise.all() for independent operations and move awaits into branches where results are actually needed.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Bundle Size for Heavy Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply code splitting strategies to this component. Identify opportunities for next/dynamic imports, conditional loading, and preloading on user interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Re-render Performance Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this component for unnecessary re-renders. Apply memoization, proper dependency arrays, derived state subscriptions, and functional setState patterns as appropriate.

output_examples.input: Review this data fetching pattern for waterfalls
output: ISSUE: Sequential await calls create waterfall (3 round trips)
FIX: Use Promise.all() for parallel execution (1 round trip)

output_examples.input: How do I lazy load a heavy code editor component
output: Use next/dynamic for lazy loading heavy components:
This defers ~300KB bundle until component renders, improving TTI.

best_practices: Start with critical waterfall elimination before medium-impact optimizations
Use React.cache() for per-request deduplication and LRU cache for cross-request caching
Extract stable callbacks using functional setState and store handlers in refs when needed
anti_patterns: Sequential await calls for independent operations
Importing entire utility libraries instead of specific functions
Passing functions directly as effect dependencies instead of primitives

faq.question: What React versions are supported?
faq.answer: Most patterns work with React 17+. Advanced patterns like startTransition, useTransition, and Suspense for data fetching require React 18+.

faq.question: Can I use this with JavaScript instead of TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes, all patterns apply to JavaScript projects. Examples use TypeScript syntax for clarity but the concepts transfer directly.

faq.question: Should I apply all 45 rules at once?
faq.answer: No. Start with CRITICAL priority rules (async waterfalls, bundle optimization) for maximum impact, then address HIGH and MEDIUM priorities progressively.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. Server component patterns like React.cache(), use cache(), and server parallel fetching are specifically designed for App Router architecture.

faq.question: How do I measure performance improvements?
faq.answer: Use Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) and React DevTools Profiler. The skill provides impact estimates like '2-10x improvement' for each pattern.

faq.question: Can Claude Code automatically apply these fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the skill context and ask Claude Code to review or refactor specific files. It will apply appropriate rules with code examples.
